paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: In the Native American Navajo nation, which sprawls across four states in the American south-west, the native language is dying. Most of its speakers are middle-aged or elderly. Although many students take classes in Navajo, the schools are run in English. Street signs, supermarket goods and even their own newspaper are all in English. Not surprisingly, linguists doubt that any native speakers of Navajo will remain in a hundred years' time.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Navajo is far from alone. Half the world's 6,800 languages are likely to vanish within two generations—that's one language lost every ten days. Never before has the planet's linguistic diversity shrunk at such a pace. "At the moment, we are heading for about three or four languages dominating the world," says Mark Pagel, an evolutionary biologist at the University of Reading. "It's a mass extinction, and whether we will ever rebound from the loss is difficult to know."

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Isolation breeds linguistic diversity: as a result, the world is peppered with languages spoken by only a few people. Only 250 languages have more than a million speakers, and at least 3,000 have fewer than 2,500. It is not necessarily these small languages that are about to disappear. Navajo is considered endangered despite having 150,000 speakers. What makes a language endangered is not just the number of speakers, but how old they are. If it is spoken by children, it is relatively safe. The critically endangered languages are those that are only spoken by the elderly, according to Michael Krauss, director of the Alaska Native Language Center, in Fairbanks.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Why do people reject the language of their parents? It begins with a crisis of confidence, when a small community finds itself alongside a larger, wealthier society, says Nicholas Ostler, of Britain's Foundation for Endangered Languages, in Bath. "People lose faith in their culture," he says. "When the next generation reaches their teens, they might not want to be inducted into the old traditions."

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: The change is not always voluntary. Quite often, governments try to kill off a minority language by banning its use in public or discouraging its use in schools, all to promote national unity. The former US policy of running Indian reservation schools in English, for example, effectively put languages such as Navajo on the danger list. But Salikoko Mufwene, who chairs the Linguistics department at the University of Chicago, argues that the deadliest weapon is not government policy but economic globalisation. "Native Americans have not lost pride in their language, but they have had to adapt to socio-economic pressures," he says. "They cannot refuse to speak English if most commercial activity is in English." But are languages worth saving? At the very least, there is a loss of data for the study of languages and their evolution, which relies on comparisons between languages, both living and dead. When an unwritten and unrecorded language disappears, it is lost to science.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Language is also intimately bound up with culture, so it may be difficult to preserve one without the other. "If a person shifts from Navajo to English, they lose something," Mufwene says. "Moreover, the loss of diversity may also deprive us of different ways of looking at the world," says Pagel. There is mounting evidence that learning a language produces physiological changes in the brain. "Your brain and mine are different from the brain of someone who speaks French, for instance," Pagel says, and this could affect our thoughts and perceptions. "The patterns and connections we make among various concepts may be structured by the linguistic habits of our community."

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: So despite linguists' best efforts, many languages will disappear over the next century. But a growing interest in cultural identity may prevent the direst predictions from coming true. "The key to fostering diversity is for people to learn their ancestral tongue, as well as the dominant language," says Doug Whalen, founder and president of the Endangered Language Fund in New Haven, Connecticut. "Most of these languages will not survive without a large degree of bilingualism," he says. In New Zealand, classes for children have slowed the erosion of Maori and rekindled interest in the language. A similar approach in Hawaii has produced about 8,000 new speakers of Polynesian languages in the past few years. In California, "apprentice" programmes have provided life support to several indigenous languages. Volunteer "apprentices" pair up with one of the last living speakers of a Native American tongue to learn a traditional skill such as basket weaving, with instruction exclusively in the endangered language. After about 300 hours of training they are generally sufficiently fluent to transmit the language to the next generation. But Mufwene says that preventing a language dying out is not the same as giving it new life by using it every day. "Preserving a language is more like preserving fruits in a jar," he says.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: However, preservation can bring a language back from the dead. There are examples of languages that have survived in written form and then been revived by later generations. But a written form is essential for this, so the mere possibility of revival has led many speakers of endangered languages to develop systems of writing where none existed before.

groups.collect: There are currently approximately 6,800 languages in the world. This great variety of languages came about largely as a result of geographical $34199$. But in today's world, factors such as government initiatives and $34200$ are contributing to a huge decrease in the number of languages. One factor which may help to ensure that some endangered languages do not die out completely is people's increasing appreciation of their $34201$. This has been encouraged through programmes of language classes for children and through 'apprentice' schemes, in which the endangered language is used as the medium of instruction to teach people a $34202$. Some speakers of endangered languages have even produced writing systems in order to help secure the survival of their mother tongue.

Questions 10-13

Choose
YES if the statement agrees with the view of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the view of the writer

questions.title: The Navajo language will die out because it currently has too few speakers.
questions.analysis: 题干中的"too few speakers"在原文中对应的是"150,000 speakers"，而"die out"对应的是"considered endangered"。原文明确指出，尽管有15万使用者，纳瓦霍语仍被认为是濒危语言，这与题干所说的"因为使用者太少而将消亡"是相反的。因此，正确答案是NO。

central_sentences.sentence: Navajo is considered endangered despite having 150,000 speakers.
central_sentences.translation: 尽管有15万使用者，纳瓦霍语仍被认为是濒危语言。

target_sen.target_sen: Navajo is considered endangered despite having 150,000 speakers.
Navajo is considered endangered despite having 150,000 speakers.

questions.title: A large number of native speakers fails to guarantee the survival of a language.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是YES，因为题干中的“large number of native speakers fails to guarantee the survival of a language”在原文中有直接对应的表达：“Navajo is considered endangered despite having 150,000 speakers”。这句话明确指出，即使有大量的使用者（15万），纳瓦霍语仍然被认为是濒危语言。这直接支持了题干的观点，即大量使用者不能保证一种语言的生存。

central_sentences.sentence: Navajo is considered endangered despite having 150,000 speakers.
central_sentences.translation: 尽管有15万使用者，纳瓦霍语仍被认为是濒危语言。

target_sen.target_sen: Navajo is considered endangered despite having 150,000 speakers.
Navajo is considered endangered despite having 150,000 speakers.

questions.title: National governments could do more to protect endangered languages.
questions.analysis: 题干中的关键词是"National governments"和"protect endangered languages"。在文章中，虽然提到了政府对语言的影响，但主要是负面的，例如"try to kill off a minority language"。文章没有讨论政府是否可以或应该做更多来保护濒危语言。因此，正确答案是NOT GIVEN，因为文章既没有支持也没有反驳题干中的观点。

central_sentences.sentence: governments try to kill off a minority language by banning its use in public or discouraging its use in schools
central_sentences.translation: 政府试图通过禁止在公共场合使用或阻止在学校使用来消灭少数民族语言。

questions.title: The loss of linguistic diversity is inevitable.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "inevitable" 和 "loss of linguistic diversity" 在原文中有对应的表达。原文中提到 "despite linguists' best efforts, many languages will disappear over the next century"，这表明尽管有努力，语言的消失是不可避免的。因此，题干中的陈述是正确的，答案为 YES。

central_sentences.translation: 因此，尽管语言学家们尽了最大的努力，许多语言将在下个世纪消失。

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: The first students to study alternative medicine at university level in Australia began their four-year, full-time course at the University of Technology, Sydney, in early 1994. Their course covered, among other therapies, acupuncture. The theory they learnt is based on the traditional Chinese explanation of this ancient healing art: that it can regulate the flow of "Qi" or energy through pathways in the body. This course reflects how far some alternative therapies have come in their struggle for acceptance by the medical establishment.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Australia has been unusual in the Western world in having a very conservative attitude to natural or alternative therapies, according to Dr Paul Laver, a lecturer in Public Health at the University of Sydney. "We've had a tradition of doctors being fairly powerful and I guess they are pretty loath to allow any pretenders to their position to come into it." In many other industrialized countries, orthodox and alternative medicines have worked "hand in glove" for years. In Europe, only orthodox doctors can prescribe herbal medicine. In Germany, plant remedies account for 10% of the national turnover of pharmaceuticals. Americans made more visits to alternative therapists than to orthodox doctors in 1990, and each year they spend about $US 12 billion on the therapies that have not been scientifically tested.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Disenchantment with orthodox medicine has seen the popularity of alternative therapies in Australia climb steadily during the past 20 years. In a 1983 national health survey, 1.9% of people said they had contacted a chiropractor, naturopath, osteopath, acupuncturist or herbalist in the two weeks prior to the survey. By 1990, this figure had risen to 2.6% of the population. The 550,000 consultations with alternative therapists reported in the 1990 survey represented about an eighth of the total number of consultations with medically qualified personnel covered by the survey, according to Dr Laver and colleagues writing in the Australian Journal of Public Health in 1993. "A better educated and less accepting public has become disillusioned with the experts in general and increasingly skeptical about science and empirically based knowledge," they said. "The high standing of professionals, including doctors, has been eroded as a consequence."

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Rather than resisting or criticizing this trend, increasing numbers of Australian doctors, particularly younger ones, are forming group practices with alternative therapists or taking courses themselves, particularly in acupuncture and herbalism. Part of the incentive was financial, Dr Laver said. "The bottom line is that most general practitioners are business people. If they see potential clientele going elsewhere, they might want to be able to offer a similar service."

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: In 1993, Dr Laver and his colleagues published a survey of 289 Sydney people who attended eight alternative therapists' practices in Sydney. These practices offered a wide range of alternative therapies from 25 therapists. Those surveyed had experienced chronic illnesses, for which orthodox medicine had been able to provide little relief. They commented that they liked the holistic approach of their alternative therapists and the friendly, concerned and detailed attention they had received. The cold, impersonal manner of orthodox doctors featured in the survey. An increasing exodus from their clinics, coupled with this and a number of other relevant surveys carried out in Australia, all pointing to orthodox doctors' inadequacies, have led mainstream doctors themselves to begin to admit they could learn from the personal style of alternative therapists. Dr Patrick Store, President of the Royal College of General Practitioners, concurs that orthodox doctors could learn a lot about bedside manner and advising patients on preventative health from alternative therapists.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: According to the Australian Journal of Public Health, 18% of patients visiting alternative therapists do so because they suffer from musculoskeletal complaints; 12% suffer from digestive problems, which is only 1% more than those suffering from emotional problems. Those suffering from respiratory complaints represent 7% of their patients, and candida sufferers represent an equal percentage. Headache sufferers and those complaining of general ill health represent 6% and 5% of patients respectively, and a further 4% see therapists for general health maintenance.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: The survey suggested that complementary medicine is probably a better term than alternative medicine. Alternative medicine appears to be an adjunct, sought in times of disenchantment when conventional medicine seems not to offer the answer.

groups.desc:
Questions 16-23
Do the following statements agree with the views of the writer in Reading Passage 2?
In boxes 16-23 on your answer sheet, write
YES if the statement agrees with the views of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the views of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

questions.title: Australians have been turning to alternative therapies in increasing numbers over the past 20 years.
questions.analysis:
- **对应关系**：
 - 题干中的 “Australians have been turning to alternative therapies in increasing numbers” 表达的是澳大利亚人转向替代疗法的数量在增加。
 - 原文中的 “the popularity of alternative therapies in Australia climb steadily” 意思是澳大利亚替代疗法的受欢迎程度在过去20年里稳步上升。
 - 通常情况下，一种疗法受欢迎程度上升，就意味着有更多的人去选择这种疗法，也就是越来越多的人转向这种疗法。所以题干和原文虽然表述方式不同，但核心意思是一致的。
- **时间对应**：题干中的 “over the past 20 years” 和原文中的 “during the past 20 years” 时间范围完全一致。

### 总结
基于以上分析，题干中的信息在原文中可以找到对应的表达，所以答案是YES。

central_sentences.sentence: Disenchantment with orthodox medicine has seen the popularity of alternative therapies in Australia climb steadily during the past 20 years.
central_sentences.translation: 对传统医学的失望使得在过去的20年里，澳大利亚的替代疗法的受欢迎程度稳步上升。

target_sen.target_sen: Disenchantment with orthodox medicine has seen the popularity of alternative therapies in Australia climb steadily during the past 20 years.

The 550,000 consultations with alternative therapists reported in the 1990 survey represented about an eighth of the total number of consultations with medically qualified personnel covered by the survey, according to Dr Laver and colleagues writing in the Australian Journal of Public Health in 1993.
Disenchantment with orthodox medicine has seen the popularity of alternative therapies in Australia climb steadily during the past 20 years.
Disenchantment with orthodox medicine has seen the popularity of alternative therapies in Australia climb steadily during the past 20 years.
Disenchantment with orthodox medicine has seen the popularity of alternative therapies in Australia climb steadily during the past 20 years.

questions.title: Between 1983 and 1990 the numbers of patients visiting alternative therapists rose to include a further 8% of the population.
questions.analysis:
这道题的正确答案是NO，题目说的是“Between 1983 and 1990 the numbers of patients visiting alternative therapists rose to include a further 8% of the population” ，意思是在1983年到1990年期间，去看替代疗法治疗师的患者数量增加到使得总人口中有额外8%的人去看了。
而文章里提到 “In a 1983 national health survey, 1.9% of people said they had contacted a chiropractor, naturopath, osteopath, acupuncturist or herbalist in the two weeks prior to the survey. By 1990, this figure had risen to 2.6% of the population” ，也就是从1983年的1.9%增长到了1990年的2.6%，实际增长的比例是2.6% - 1.9% = 0.7%，这和题干中说的额外8%相差可大啦。
所以题干的信息和文章里的信息是相反的，正确答案是NO

central_sentences.sentence: In a 1983 national health survey, 1.9% of people said they had contacted a chiropractor, naturopath, osteopath, acupuncturist or herbalist in the two weeks prior to the survey. By 1990, this figure had risen to 2.6% of the population.
central_sentences.translation: 在1983年的全国健康调查中，1.9%的人表示他们在调查前的两周内联系过脊椎按摩师、自然疗法师、整骨师、针灸师或草药师。到1990年，这一比例上升到了2.6%。

target_sen.target_sen: In a 1983 national health survey, 1.9% of people said they had contacted a chiropractor, naturopath, osteopath, acupuncturist or herbalist in the two weeks prior to the survey.
By 1990, this figure had risen to 2.6% of the population.

syn_ary.synonym: 1.9% of people said they had contacted a chiropractor, naturopath, osteopath, acupuncturist or herbalist

target_sen.target_sen: In a 1983 national health survey, 1.9% of people said they had contacted a chiropractor, naturopath, osteopath, acupuncturist or herbalist in the two weeks prior to the survey.

syn_ary.synonym: this figure had risen to 2.6% of the population

target_sen.target_sen: By 1990, this figure had risen to 2.6% of the population.

locating_words.locating_word: numbers of patients visiting alternative therapists

questions.title: The 1990 survey related to 550,000 consultations with alternative therapists.
questions.analysis:
正确答案是YES，题干说 “The 1990 survey related to 550,000 consultations with alternative therapists”，也就是1990年的调查涉及550,000次与替代疗法治疗师的咨询。
咱们再看看原文，里面提到 “The 550,000 consultations with alternative therapists reported in the 1990 survey represented about an eighth of the total number of consultations with medically qualified personnel covered by the survey” 。这清楚地表明了在1990年的调查里，确实有550,000次与替代疗法治疗师的咨询，虽然这550,000次只是总咨询次数的八分之一，但题干只是说调查涉及这550,000次咨询，并没有说这是全部的调查结果。所以呀，题干信息和原文是相符的，正确答案是YES

central_sentences.sentence: The 550,000 consultations with alternative therapists reported in the 1990 survey represented about an eighth of the total number of consultations with medically qualified personnel covered by the survey
central_sentences.translation: 1990年调查报告中提到的与替代疗法治疗师的550,000次咨询，占调查中有医疗资格人员总咨询次数的八分之一。

target_sen.target_sen: The 550,000 consultations with alternative therapists reported in the 1990 survey represented about an eighth of the total number of consultations with medically qualified personnel covered by the survey, according to Dr Laver and colleagues writing in the Australian Journal of Public Health in 1993.

The 550,000 consultations with alternative therapists reported in the 1990 survey represented about an eighth of the total number of consultations with medically qualified personnel covered by the survey, according to Dr Laver and colleagues writing in the Australian Journal of Public Health in 1993.

questions.title: In the past, Australians had a higher opinion, of doctors than they do today.
questions.analysis:
这道题答案是YES，题干说 “In the past, Australians had a higher opinion of doctors than they do today”，意思是过去澳大利亚人对医生的评价比现在高。
在文中 “The high standing of professionals, including doctors, has been eroded as a consequence” 这句话，“high standing” 就是高地位、高评价的意思，“has been eroded” 表明这种高评价已经被削弱了。这就意味着过去医生是有高评价的，而现在这种高评价没那么高了，也就是过去澳大利亚人对医生的评价比现在高，和题干表述一致。所以呀，这道题答案就是YES

central_sentences.sentence: The high standing of professionals, including doctors, has been eroded as a consequence.
central_sentences.translation: 包括医生在内的专业人士的高地位因此被削弱了。

target_sen.target_sen: "The high standing of professionals, including doctors, has been eroded as a consequence."
"The high standing of professionals, including doctors, has been eroded as a consequence."
"The high standing of professionals, including doctors, has been eroded as a consequence."

questions.title: Some Australian doctors are retraining in alternative therapies.
questions.analysis:
答案是YES，首先呢，题干说“Some Australian doctors are retraining in alternative therapies”，也就是一些澳大利亚医生正在接受替代疗法的再培训。
再看看原文，提到“increasing numbers of Australian doctors, particularly younger ones, are forming group practices with alternative therapists or taking courses themselves, particularly in acupuncture and herbalism” 。这里明确说了越来越多的澳大利亚医生，尤其是年轻医生，要么和替代疗法治疗师合作开诊所，要么自己去上相关课程，特别是针灸和草药学方面的课程。
咱们来分析一下，“taking courses”其实就是在学习替代疗法相关的知识呀，这本质上就是一种再培训的行为呢。虽然原文没直接用“retraining”这个词，但做的事情就是在接受替代疗法的再培训呀。所以从原文是能找到和题干对应的信息的，答案确实应该是YES

central_sentences.sentence: increasing numbers of Australian doctors, particularly younger ones, are forming group practices with alternative therapists or taking courses themselves, particularly in acupuncture and herbalism.
central_sentences.translation: 越来越多的澳大利亚医生，特别是年轻医生，正在与替代疗法治疗师组成联合诊所，或者自己参加课程，特别是针灸和草药学课程。

target_sen.target_sen: Rather than resisting or criticizing this trend, increasing numbers of Australian doctors, particularly younger ones, are forming group practices with alternative therapists or taking courses themselves, particularly in acupuncture and herbalism.

Rather than resisting or criticizing this trend, increasing numbers of Australian doctors, particularly younger ones, are forming group practices with alternative therapists or taking courses themselves, particularly in acupuncture and herbalism.

Rather than resisting or criticizing this trend, increasing numbers of Australian doctors, particularly younger ones, are forming group practices with alternative therapists or taking courses themselves, particularly in acupuncture and herbalism.

questions.title: Alternative therapists earn higher salaries than doctors.
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是NOT GIVEN，因为文章中没有提供关于替代疗法治疗师和医生薪水比较的信息。虽然文章提到了替代疗法的增长和受欢迎程度，但这并不直接反映治疗师的薪水水平。因此，无法判断替代疗法治疗师是否比医生赚得更多。

central_sentences.reason: 无“alternative therapists”与“doctors”薪资对比信息。
central_sentences.analysis: 只提医生出于financial incentive，并未比较收入高低，NOT GIVEN。
sentences: Part of the incentive was financial, Dr Laver said.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: earn higher salaries -> financial (未比较); therapists vs doctors -> 未提及

central_sentences.sentence: Part of the incentive was financial, Dr Laver said.

questions.title: The 1993 Sydney survey involved 289 patients who visited alternative therapists for acupuncture treatment.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“289名患者访问替代疗法治疗师进行针灸治疗”，而原文中提到的是“289名悉尼人访问了提供广泛替代疗法的治疗师”，并没有特指针灸治疗。因此，题干中的信息与原文不符，答案应为NO。

central_sentences.sentence: In 1993, Dr Laver and his colleagues published a survey of 289 Sydney people who attended eight alternative therapists" practices in Sydney. These practices offered a wide range of alternative therapies from 25 therapists. Those
central_sentences.translation: 1993年，Laver博士和他的同事们发表了一项调查，调查了289名悉尼人，他们访问了悉尼的八个替代疗法治疗师的诊所。这些诊所提供了25位治疗师的广泛替代疗法。

target_sen.target_sen: In 1993, Dr Laver and his colleagues published a survey of 289 Sydney people who attended eight alternative therapists" practices in Sydney.
In 1993, Dr Laver and his colleagues published a survey of 289 Sydney people who attended eight alternative therapists" practices in Sydney.
These practices offered a wide range of alternative therapies from 25 therapists.

questions.title: All the patients in the 1993 Sydney survey had long-term medical complaints.
questions.analysis:
答案是YES，题干说“All the patients in the 1993 Sydney survey had long - term medical complaints”，也就是1993年悉尼调查里所有患者都有长期医疗问题。
原文提到“Those surveyed had experience chronic illnesses”，这里“Those surveyed”指的就是参与调查的人，“chronic illnesses”就是慢性疾病呀，慢性疾病可不就是长期的健康问题嘛。
在这个语境下，参与调查的人其实就等同于题干里说的“所有患者”，因为说的就是这次悉尼调查里的情况。所以题干的信息和原文是相符的，答案确实应该是YES

central_sentences.sentence: Those surveyed had experience chronic illnesses, for which orthodox medicine had been able to provide little relief.
central_sentences.translation: 那些被调查的人经历过慢性疾病，而正统医学几乎无法提供缓解。

target_sen.target_sen: An increasing exodus from their clinics, coupled with this and a number of other relevant surveys carried out in Australia, all pointing to orthodox doctors" inadequacies, have led mainstream doctors themselves to begin to admit they could learn from the personal style of alternative therapists.
Those surveyed had experience chronic illnesses, for which orthodox medicine had been able to provide little relief.

groups.desc:
Questions 24-26

Complete the vertical axis in the chart below.
Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer.

questions.analysis: 题目要求填写图表中的垂直轴标签。根据文章内容，图表的标题是“患者就诊的医疗投诉”。在文章中提到，12%的患者因为消化问题就诊，这仅比那些因为情感问题就诊的患者多1%。因此，情感问题的比例是11%，与图表中第24题的位置相符，所以正确答案是“emotional problems”。

central_sentences.sentence: According to the Australian Journal of Public Health, 18% of patients visiting alternative therapists do so because they suffer from muscular-skeletal complaints; 12% suffer from digestive problems, which is only 1% more than those suffering from emotional problems.
central_sentences.translation: 根据《澳大利亚公共卫生杂志》，18%的患者因为肌肉骨骼问题而就诊替代疗法治疗师；12%的患者因为消化问题就诊，这仅比那些因为情感问题就诊的患者多1%。

target_sen.target_sen: According to the Australian Journal of Public Health, 18% of patients visiting alternative therapists do so because they suffer from muscular-skeletal complaints; 12% suffer from digestive problems, which is only 1% more than those suffering from emotional problems.

questions.analysis: 第25题的正确答案是“headaches”，因为图表中显示的百分比是6%，而文章中明确提到“Headache sufferers”占6%。通过匹配图表中的百分比和文章中的具体描述，可以确定答案。

questions.analysis: 正确答案是 "general ill health"，因为图表中26题的位置对应的百分比是5%，而文章中与5%对应的医疗投诉类型是 "general ill health"。通过对比百分比和关键词定位，可以确定答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Headache sufferers and those complaining of general ill health represent 6% and 5% of patients respectively, and a further 4% see therapists for general health maintenance.

content.title: PLAY IS A SERIOUS BUSINESS
content.summary: {'A': '玩耍并非只是轻松愉快的消遣，它可能让动物付出生命代价且消耗大量能量，但必有其存在的原因。', 'B': '最新观点认为玩耍是为了发展大脑而进化的，且关于玩耍的一些常见解释近年来受到质疑。', 'C': '锻炼理论认为如果玩耍是为了锻炼肌肉或耐力训练，其效果应是持久的，但实际并非如此。', 'D': '技能训练假说认为动物玩耍是在练习成年所需技能，但研究发现并非这么简单。', 'E': '研究表明大脑大小和玩耍性在哺乳动物中普遍存在强正相关。', 'F': '动物幼崽玩耍阶段的时间安排提供了重要线索，与大脑发育的敏感时期相关。', 'G': '玩耍时的行为比想象的更具变化性和不可预测性，能激活大脑许多不同部分。', 'H': '玩耍不仅涉及大脑更多区域，还似乎能激活更高认知过程，可能增强创造力。', 'I': '思考当今社会儿童养育方式可能带来的影响，如早期教育和应试导向可能减少玩耍。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Playing is a serious business. Children engrossed in a make-believe world, fox cubs play-fighting or kittens teasing a ball of string aren't just having fun. Play may look like a carefree and exuberant way to pass the time before the hard work of adulthood comes along, but there's much more to it than that. For a start, play can even cost animals their lives. Eighty per cent of deaths among juvenile fur seals occur because playing pups fail to spot predators approaching. It is also extremely expensive in terms of energy. Playful young animals use around two or three per cent of their energy cavorting, and in children that figure can be closer to fifteen per cent. "Even two or three per cent is huge," says John Byers of Idaho University. "You just don't find animals wasting energy like that," he adds. There must be a reason.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: But if play is not simply a developmental hiccup, as biologists once thought, why did it evolve? The latest idea suggests that play has evolved to build big brains. In other words, playing makes you intelligent. Playfulness, it seems, is common only among mammals, although a few of the larger-brained birds also indulge. Animals at play often use unique signs - tail-wagging in dogs, for example - to indicate that activity superficially resembling adult behavior is not really in earnest. A popular explanation of play has been that it helps juveniles develop the skills they will need to hunt, mate and socialise as adults. Another has been that it allows young animals to get in shape for adult life by improving their respiratory endurance. Both these ideas have been questioned in recent years.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Take the exercise theory. If play evolved to build muscle or as a kind of endurance training, then you would expect to see permanent benefits. But Byers points out that the benefits of increased exercise disappear rapidly after training stops, so any improvement in endurance resulting from juvenile play would be lost by adulthood. "If the function of play was to get into shape," says Byers, "the optimum time for playing would depend on when it was most advantageous for the young of a particular species to do so. But it doesn't work like that." Across species, play tends to peak about halfway through the suckling stage and then decline.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Then there's the skills-training hypothesis. At first glance, playing animals do appear to be practising the complex manoeuvres they will need in adulthood. But a closer inspection reveals this interpretation as too simplistic. In one study, behavioural ecologist Tim Caro, from the University of California, looked at the predatory play of kittens and their predatory behaviour when they reached adulthood. He found that the way the cats played had no significant effect on their hunting prowess in later life.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Earlier this year, Sergio Pellis of Lethbridge University, Canada, reported that there is a strong positive link between brain size and playfulness among mammals in general. Comparing measurements for fifteen orders of mammal, he and his team found larger brains (for a given body size) are linked to greater playfulness. The converse was also found to be true. Robert Barton of Durham University believes that, because large brains are more sensitive to developmental stimuli than smaller brains, they require more play to help mould them for adulthood. “I concluded it's to do with learning, and with the importance of environmental data to the brain during development,” he says.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: According to Byers, the timing of the playful stage in young animals provides an important clue to what's going on. If you plot the amount of time a juvenile devotes to play each day over the course of its development, you discover a pattern typically associated with a 'sensitive period' - a brief development window during which the brain can actually be modified in ways that are not possible earlier or later in life. Think of the relative ease with which young children - but not infants or adults - absorb language. Other researchers have found that play in cats, rats and mice is at its most intense just as this "window of opportunity" reaches its peak.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: "People have not paid enough attention to the amount of the brain activated by play," says Marc Bekoff from Colorado University. Bekoff studied coyote pups at play and found that the kind of behaviour involved was markedly more variable and unpredictable than that of adults. Such behaviour activates many different parts of the brain, he reasons. Bekoff likens it to a behavioural kaleidoscope, with animals at play jumping rapidly between activities. "They use behaviour from a lot of different contexts - predation, aggression, reproduction," he says. "Their developing brain is getting all sorts of stimulation.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Not only is more of the brain involved in play than was suspected, but it also seems to activate higher cognitive processes. "There's enormous cognitive involvement in play", says Bekoff. He points out that play often involves complex assessments of playmates, ideas of reciprocity and the use of specialised signals and rules. He believes that play creates a brain that has greater behavioural flexibility and improved potential for learning later in life. The idea is backed up by the work of Stephen Siviy of Gettysburg College. Siviy studied how bouts of play affected the brain's levels of a particular chemical associated with the stimulation and growth of nerve cells. He was surprised by the extent of the activation. "Play just lights everything up," he says. By allowing link-ups between brain areas that might not normally communicate with each other, play may enhance creativity.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: What might further experimentation suggest about the way children are raised in many societies today? We already know that rat pups denied the chance to play grow smaller brain components and fail to develop the ability to apply social rules when they interact with their peers. With schooling beginning earlier and becoming increasingly exam-orientated, play is likely to get even less of a look-in. Who knows what the result of that will be?

groups.desc:
Questions 27-32

Reading Passage 3 has nine paragraphs labelled A-I.
Which paragraph contains the following information?
Choose the correct letter A-I.

NB You may use any letter more than once.

questions.title: the way play causes unusual connections in the brain which are beneficial
questions.analysis: 段落H明确提到玩耍如何通过允许大脑中不同区域之间的连接来增强创造力，这正是题干中“unusual connections in the brain which are beneficial”的意思。因此，段落H是最符合题干要求的答案。

central_sentences.sentence: By allowing link-ups between brain areas that might not normally communicate with each other, play may enhance creativity.
central_sentences.translation: 通过允许大脑中通常不会相互交流的区域之间的连接，玩耍可能会增强创造力。

syn_ary.synonym: link-ups between brain areas that might not normally communicate with each other

target_sen.target_sen: By allowing link-ups between brain areas that might not normally communicate with each other, play may enhance creativity.
By allowing link-ups between brain areas that might not normally communicate with each other, play may enhance creativity.

questions.title: insights from recording how much time young animals spend playing
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息 "recording how much time young animals spend playing" 在段落F中有直接对应的表达 "If you plot the amount of time a juvenile devotes to play each day over the course of its development"。这里的 "plot" 对应 "recording"，"the amount of time" 对应 "how much time"，"juvenile" 对应 "young animals"，"devotes to play" 对应 "spend playing"。因此，段落F是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: If you plot the amount of time a juvenile devotes to play each day over the course of its development
central_sentences.translation: 如果你绘制一张图表，记录一只幼兽在其发育过程中每天花在玩耍上的时间。

target_sen.target_sen: If you plot the amount of time a juvenile devotes to play each day over the course of its development, you discover a pattern typically associated with a 'sensitive period" - a brief development window during which the brain can actually be modified in ways that are not possible earlier or later in life.

If you plot the amount of time a juvenile devotes to play each day over the course of its development, you discover a pattern typically associated with a 'sensitive period" - a brief development window during which the brain can actually be modified in ways that are not possible earlier or later in life.

questions.title: a description of the physical hazards that can accompany play
questions.analysis: 段落A明确描述了玩耍可能带来的物理危险。文中提到："Eighty per cent of deaths among juvenile fur seals occur because playing pups fail to spot predators approaching." 这句话直接对应了题干中描述的"physical hazards that can accompany play"。它指出了幼年海豹在玩耍时可能面临的致命危险（被捕食者袭击）。其他段落虽然也讨论了玩耍，但都没有直接提到玩耍带来的物理危险。因此，段落A是最符合题干要求的答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Eighty per cent of deaths among juvenile fur seals occur because playing pups fail to spot predators approaching.
central_sentences.translation: 80%的幼年海豹死亡是因为玩耍的小海豹未能发现接近的捕食者。

target_sen.target_sen: For a start, play can even cost animals their lives.
Eighty per cent of deaths among juvenile fur seals occur because playing pups fail to spot predators approaching.
Eighty per cent of deaths among juvenile fur seals occur because playing pups fail to spot predators approaching.

questions.title: a description of the mental activities which are exercised and developed during play
questions.analysis: 段落H详细描述了玩耍过程中涉及的认知活动，包括复杂的评估、互惠概念和使用特殊信号和规则。这些活动都属于“mental activities”，并且段落H还提到玩耍如何“improved potential for learning later in life”，这与题干中的“exercised and developed”相对应。因此，段落H是最符合题干要求的答案。

central_sentences.sentence: "There's enormous cognitive involvement in play", says Bekoff. He points out that play often involves complex assessments of playmates, ideas of reciprocity and the use of specialised signals and rules. He believes that play creates a brain that has greater behavioural flexibility and improved potential for learning later in life.
central_sentences.translation: “玩耍中有大量的认知参与，”贝科夫说。他指出，玩耍通常涉及对玩伴的复杂评估、互惠概念以及使用特殊信号和规则。他认为，玩耍创造了一个具有更大行为灵活性和更好学习潜力的大脑。

target_sen.target_sen: "There's enormous cognitive involvement in play", says Bekoff.
Not only is more of the brain involved in play than was suspected, but it also seems to activate higher cognitive processes.
He believes that play creates a brain that has greater behavioural flexibility and improved potential for learning later in life.
By allowing link-ups between brain areas that might not normally communicate with each other, play may enhance creativity.

questions.title: the possible effects that a reduction in play opportunities will have on humans
questions.analysis: 题干中的“reduction in play opportunities”在原文中对应“play is likely to get even less of a look-in”，而“possible effects”对应“Who knows what the result of that will be?”。段落I明确提到了玩耍机会减少的可能性及其潜在影响，因此是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: With schooling beginning earlier and becoming increasingly exam-orientated, play is likely to get even less of a look-in. Who knows what the result of that will be?
central_sentences.translation: 随着上学时间的提前和考试导向的增加，玩耍的机会可能会越来越少。谁知道这会带来什么结果呢？

syn_ary.synonym: play is likely to get even less of a look-in

target_sen.target_sen: With schooling beginning earlier and becoming increasingly exam-orientated, play is likely to get even less of a look-in.

syn_ary.synonym: Who knows what the result of that will be?

target_sen.target_sen: Who knows what the result of that will be?

questions.title: the classes of animals for which play is important
questions.analysis: 题目问的是“哪些类别的动物玩耍很重要”，段落B明确指出了哺乳动物和部分大脑较大的鸟类会玩耍，这与题干中的“classes of animals”和“important”直接对应。因此，段落B是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Playfulness, it seems, is common only among mammals, although a few of the larger-brained birds also indulge.
central_sentences.translation: 看起来，只有哺乳动物中常见玩耍行为，尽管一些大脑较大的鸟类也会参与其中。

target_sen.target_sen: Playfulness, it seems, is common only among mammals, although a few of the larger-brained birds also indulge.
Playfulness, it seems, is common only among mammals, although a few of the larger-brained birds also indulge.

groups.title: Which THREE ways are mentioned by the writer of the text?

Questions 33-35

Choose THREE letters.

The list below gives some ways of regarding play.

an activity intended to build up strength for adulthoood

questions.analysis: 这道题目要求学生从文章中识别出三种关于玩耍的主要观点。通过仔细阅读，特别是Section B，学生可以找到这三种观点的描述。重要的是要理解这些观点是文章讨论的主要内容，而不是随意提及的概念。

central_sentences.sentence: it helps juveniles develop the skills they will need to hunt, mate and socialise as adults
central_sentences.translation: 它帮助幼年动物发展他们在成年后需要的狩猎、交配和社交技能。

central_sentences.sentence: it allows young animals to get in shape for adult life by improving their respiratory endurance
central_sentences.translation: 它让幼年动物通过提高呼吸耐力为成年生活做好准备。

central_sentences.sentence: play has evolved to build big brains
central_sentences.translation: 玩耍已经进化为发展大脑。

groups.title: List of Findings
groups.desc:
Questions 36-40

Look at the following researchers (Questions 36-40) and the list of findings below.
Match each researcher with the correct finding.
Write the correct letter and move into the gap.

list.text: There is a link between a specific substance in the brain and playing
Play provides input concerning physical surroundings
Varieties of play can be matched to different stages of evolutionary history
There is a tendency for mammals with smaller brains to play less
Play is not a form of fitness training for the future
Some species of larger brained birds engage in play
A wide range of activities are combined during play
Play is a method of teaching survival techniques

questions.title: Robert Barton
questions.analysis: Robert Barton的研究发现是关于游戏对大脑发育过程中环境数据的重要性，这与选项B“Play provides input concerning physical surroundings”直接对应。题干中的“input concerning physical surroundings”在原文中被替换为“environmental data”，这两个短语意思相同，都是指环境数据或信息。

central_sentences.sentence: “I concluded it's to do with learning, and with the importance of environmental data to the brain during development,” he says.
central_sentences.translation: “我得出的结论是，这与学习有关，并且在大脑发育过程中，环境数据非常重要，”他说。

target_sen.target_sen: Robert Barton of Durham University believes that, because large brains are more sensitive to developmental stimuli than smaller brains, they require more play to help mould them for adulthood.

questions.title: Marc Bekoff
questions.analysis: Marc Bekoff 的研究表明，玩耍中的动物会在多种活动之间快速切换，这种行为比成年动物更加多变和不可预测。这与选项 G 中的 "a wide range of activities are combined during play" 完全吻合。

central_sentences.sentence: Bekoff studied coyote pups at play and found that the kind of behaviour involved was markedly more variable and unpredictable than that of adults. Such behaviour activates many different parts of the brain, he reasons. Bekoff likens it to a behavioural kaleidoscope, with animals at play jumping rapidly between activities. "They use behaviour from a lot of different contexts - predation, aggression, reproduction/ he says. "Their
central_sentences.translation: 贝科夫研究了玩耍中的郊狼幼崽，发现它们的行为比成年郊狼更加多变和不可预测。他认为这种行为激活了大脑的许多不同部分。贝科夫将其比作行为万花筒，玩耍中的动物在不同活动之间快速切换。他说：“它们使用来自许多不同背景的行为——捕食、攻击、繁殖。”

target_sen.target_sen: "People have not paid enough attention to the amount of the brain activated by play," says Marc Bekoff from Colorado University.

questions.title: John Byers
questions.analysis: John Byers在原文中明确否定了“游戏是为了健身”的观点。他指出，如果游戏的功能是为了健身，那么游戏的最佳时间应该取决于对某个特定物种的幼崽最有利的时间，但事实并非如此。这与选项E“Play is not a form of fitness training for the future”直接对应。

central_sentences.sentence: If the function of play was to get into shape," says Byers, "the optimum time for playing would depend on when it was most advantageous for the young of a particular species to do so, But it doesn"t work like that." Across
central_sentences.translation: “如果游戏的功能是为了健身，”Byers说，“那么游戏的最佳时间应该取决于对某个特定物种的幼崽最有利的时间。但事实并非如此。”

questions.title: Sergio Pellis
questions.analysis: 答案D是正确的，因为文章明确指出了大脑较大的哺乳动物更爱玩耍，而大脑较小的哺乳动物则玩耍较少。这与选项D的描述完全一致。

central_sentences.sentence: Sergio Pellis of Lethbridge University, Canada, reported that there is a strong positive link between brain size and playfulness among mammals in general. Comparing measurements for fifteen orders of mammal, he and his team found larger brains (for a given body size) are linked to greater playfulness. The converse was also found to be true.
central_sentences.translation: 加拿大莱斯布里奇大学的Sergio Pellis报告称，哺乳动物的大脑大小和玩耍的积极性之间存在强烈的正相关关系。通过比较15种哺乳动物的测量数据，他和他的团队发现，较大的大脑（相对于身体大小）与更大的玩耍积极性有关。反之亦然。

syn_ary.synonym: Sergio Pellis of Lethbridge University, Canada

target_sen.target_sen: Earlier this year, Sergio Pellis of Lethbridge University, Canada, reported that there is a strong positive link between brain size and playfulness among mammals in general.

questions.title: Stephen Siviy
questions.analysis: Stephen Siviy的研究发现是关于玩耍如何影响大脑中某种特定化学物质的水平，这与选项A中的“specific substance in the brain”直接对应。因此，正确答案是A。

central_sentences.sentence: Siviy studied how bouts of play affected the brain's levels of a particular chemical associated with the stimulation and growth of nerve cells. He
central_sentences.translation: Stephen Siviy研究了玩耍对大脑中与神经细胞的刺激和生长相关的特定化学物质水平的影响。

target_sen.target_sen: The idea is backed up by the work of Stephen Siviy of Gettysburg College.
